author: mark
text:
Distinct, path-aware discovery errors

The five NotInitialized sites all reported "not initialized. Run
`bl init`", a catch-all that answered neither "am I in the wrong
directory?" nor "is this an untracked repo?" nor "is the store
broken?". For an agent dropped into an unfamiliar tree these are
different questions with different fixes, and collapsing them is what
hid the bare-repo discovery bug during debugging.

NotInitialized now carries a NotInitKind discriminating: no .balls/
anywhere on the walk (with the resolved search chain, a → b → /),
git repo without .balls/, .balls/ present but state worktree gone
(points at `bl repair`), no-git store unusable (stealth dir missing
vs needs-git), and config file absent. find_balls_root accumulates
the visited paths instead of discarding them. Every message keeps
the `not initialized` substring so existing scrapers/tests stay
green; terse BallError constructors keep store.rs at its line limit.
